title: Complete Medieval History – GV Sir Headshot Series
source: GvKaksha / Eduquity Pattern – SSC CGL/CHSL/MTS/Delhi Police 2026

table.sub_areas: Mahmud of Ghazni, Muhammad Ghori, Prithviraj Chauhan, Battles of Tarain
Qutub-ud-din Aibak, Iltutmish, Razia Sultan, Balban, Qutub Minar, Iqta system
Alauddin Khilji, Market reforms, Malik Kafur, Southern campaigns, Mongol invasions
Ghiyasuddin, Muhammad Bin Tughlaq, Firoz Shah Tughlaq, Token currency, Capital transfer
Bahlol Lodhi, Sikander Lodhi, Ibrahim Lodhi, First Battle of Panipat
Kharaz, Jizya, Khums, Zakat, Iqta, Departments (Diwans)
Harihara, Bukka, Krishnadeva Raya, Battle of Talikota, Gol Gumbaz, Amaram
Panipat, Khanwa, Chausa, Sher Shah Suri, Baburnama, Humayun-Nama
GT Road, Rupaya, Sarkar, Mauza, Zabti, Sasaram Tomb
Mansab system, Bairam Khan, Buland Darwaza, Fatehpur Sikri, Din-i-Ilahi, Navratnas

table.topic: Mughal Empire – Jahangir, Shah Jahan, Aurangzeb
table.sub_areas: Nur Jahan, Taj Mahal, Red Fort, Jizya reimposed, Deccan campaigns, Decline
Indo-Islamic style, Qutub Minar, Taj Mahal, Amir Khusrau, Bhakti & Sufi movements

subtopics.name: Mahmud of Ghazni
content: Birth: 971 AD in Zabulistan (Afghanistan); He was both Turk & Afghan
Father: Sabuktigin (Turk); Mother: Local woman of Iran
Reign Period: 998–1030 AD; Capital: Ghazni (Afghanistan) #imp.2024
Attacked India 17 times between 1000–1027 AD
1001 – Battle of Peshawar: Pala king Jaipal defeated #imp
1005 – Bhatia Attack
1006 – Multan Attack; also defeated Anandapala (son of Jaipal) #imp
1018 – Plundered Mathura Temples & attacked Kannauj ruler Rajyapal #imp
1021 – Conquered Kannauj by defeating Chandela Gaur
1025 – Plundered Somnath Temple, Gujarat #imp
1027 – Attack Against Jaat Community
1030 – Died due to Malaria and Tuberculosis
famous_writers_at_ghaznavid_court: Al-Biruni – wrote Kitab-ul-Hind or Tehqiq-i-Hind (in Arabic)
Firdausi – wrote Shahnama (Full name: Abul-Qasim Firdowsi Tusi)
Muhammad Ibn Utbi – wrote Al-Kitab al-Yamini (in Arabic)

subtopics.name: Prithviraj Chauhan
content: Real Name: Prithviraj III / Rai Pithora
Dynasty: Chahamana; Ruled: Sapadalabha
Father/Mother: Someshwar Chauhan / Karpouri
Court Painter: Anirudh
Poet friend: Chandbardai (wrote Prithviraj Raso) #imp.2024
1191 – Battle of Tarain I: Prithviraj Won; Ghori attacked Tabarhinda (Bhatinda) #imp
1192 – Battle of Tarain II: Ghori Won #imp
1194 – Battle of Chadawar: Ghori defeated Jai Chand #imp
Ananga Pala Tomar founded Delhi in 1052 AD & ruled before Prithviraj Chauhan
Bijoliya Inscription reveals Chauhans are Vats Gotriya Brahman #imp.2024
PYQ: Chahamana ruler who defeated Ghori in 1191 = Prithviraja III (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Prithviraj Chauhan's reign ended in 1192 AD (Ans: B – SSC CGL)

subtopics.name: Muhammad Ghori (Muhammad Bin Sang)
content: 1175 – Battle of Multan: Ghori defeated Moolraj II #imp
1178 – Battle of Mt. Abu: Bhim Dev II won #imp
1191 – Battle of Tarain I: Defeated by Prithviraj Chauhan
1192 – Battle of Tarain II: Ghori won; end of Chahamana rule

subtopics.name: Overview – 5 Dynasties
content: Slave (Mamluk) Dynasty: 1206–1290
Khilji Dynasty: 1290–1320
Tughlaq Dynasty: 1320–1414
Sayyid Dynasty: 1414–1450
Lodhi Dynasty: 1451–1526
True arch and dome in India first introduced by: Delhi Sultanate (Ans: B – SSC CGL)
Official language of Delhi Sultanate: Persian

subtopics.name: Qutub-ud-din Aibak (1206–1210)
content: Founder of Slave Dynasty; first to introduce Turkish culture in Delhi
He never accepted title of 'Sultan' #imp; Titles accepted: Malik / SipahSalar / Lakh Baksh
Capital: Lahore
Built Qutub Minar (Height 73 m, Indo-Islamic Architecture of Red & buff Sandstone)
Built Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque at Delhi (1192–1198 AD) – India's 1st Mosque
Built Adhai Din Ka Jhopra at Ajmer
Patronised Hasan-un-Nizami (author of 'Taj-ul-Massir' – 1st official history of Delhi Sultanate)
Also patronised Fakhr-i-Mudabbir (author of 'Shajara-yi Ansab')
1210 – Died while playing Chaugan/Polo #imp
PYQ: Qutub Minar base diameter = 14.32 metres (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Qutub Minar – Indo-Islamic style (Ans: B – Steno Exam)

subtopics.name: Shams-ud-Din Iltutmish (1211–1236)
content: Defeated Aram Shah & occupied throne of Delhi
Real Founder of Delhi Sultanate #imp
First Sultan recognised by the Caliph of Baghdad #imp
Shifted capital from Lahore to Delhi
Established Chahalgani/Chalisa (Group of 40 Nobles) #imp.2024
Completed construction of Qutub Minar #imp
Launched Iqta System – land division between Sultan and nobility; Khalisa (Sultan's land) vs Iqta #imp
Introduced 2 coins: Tanka (silver) & Jital (copper) #imp.2024
Known as 'Father of Tomb Construction'; built Sultan Garhi (Qutub Complex, Delhi)
Minhaj-ul-Siraj (author of Tabaqat-i-Nasiri) was one of his patrons
Nominated daughter Razia as successor; initiated hereditary succession
PYQ: Chahalgani formed during reign of = Iltutmish (Ans: B – SSC CGL)
PYQ: Iltutmish succeeded Aibak, later followed by daughter Razia = Only 1 correct (Ans: A – SSC Phase XIII)

subtopics.name: Razia Sultan (1236–1240) #imp.2024
content: First and Last Woman Muslim ruler of Delhi
Did not accept 'Parda' #imp
Wore 'Quba' (Blazer) and 'Qula' (Cap) #imp
Commander: Jamal-ud-din Yaqut (Abyssinian Slave)
Altunia killed Yaqut & imprisoned Razia in Qila Mubarak #imp
Razia later married Malik Ikhtiaruddin Altunia (Governor of Bathinda)
Coins: Silver Tanka, Billon Jital, and Gold; some bore title 'Umdat-ul Niswan'
Minhaj-ul-Siraj acknowledged Razia was more capable than her brothers
PYQ: Only woman monarch of Delhi Sultanate (1236–1240) = Razia Sultana (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Chronicler who acknowledged Razia's capability = Minhaj-us-Siraj (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)

subtopics.name: Ghiyas-ud-din Balban (1266–1287)
content: Followed 'Blood and Iron' policy
Titles: Ulugh Khan, Naib-i-Mamlikat, Nasir-amir-ul-momin, Zil-i-ilahi, Kingmaker
Killed/suppressed Chahalgani (Group of 40) #imp
Introduced Nauroz Culture
Introduced Sijdah (Zaminbosi) and Paibos customs
Court Poets: Amir Khusrau & Amir Hasan
Separated Diwan-i-Wizarat (Finance) from Diwan-i-Arz (Defence Department)
Appointed son Bughra Khan as Governor of Bengal after defeating Tughril Beg Khan
His Prince Muhammad Khan died fighting Mongols
PYQ: Chronological order of Slave Dynasty rulers: Qutb-ud-din Aibak → Iltutmish → Razia Sultan → Balban (Ans: B – SSC CGL)

subtopics.name: Jalal-ud-din Firoz Khilji (1290–1296)
content: Founder of Khilji Dynasty
Killed by his nephew Alauddin Khilji

subtopics.name: Alauddin Khilji (1296–1316)
content: Most effective ruler of Khilji Dynasty
Titles: Alexander Second (Sikander-i-Sani), Yamin-ul-Khilafat, Nasir-e-amir-ul-momini
Real Name: Ali Gurshasp
Appointed as Amir-i-Tuzuk (Master of Ceremonies) and Ariz-i-Mumalik (Minister of Law) by Jalaluddin
Reformed Army (Recruitment/Salary); Introduced Chehra system (I.D. Card) and 'Daag & Huliya'
Introduced land measurement unit 'Biswa'
Introduced Market Control System; Fixed land revenue in cash
3 Primary Taxes: Kharaj (50% Land tax), Gharai (Housing Tax), Charai (Cattle tax); Jizya from non-Muslims
Malik Kafur ('Hazaar Dinari') – captured by Nusrat Khan in Gujarat conquest
Reorganised spy services (Barids); 'Kingship Knows No Kinship'
Court Poets: Amir Khusrau (called Tuti-i-Hind) and Mir Hasan Dehlavi
Amir Khusrau – first poet to write about Jauhar in Persian
Created post of Diwan-i-Mustakhraj
Markets controlled by Diwan-i-Riyasat (register Merchants) & Shahana-i-Mandi (Market Controller)
Ziauddin Barani wrote Tarikh-i-FiruzShahi (about 6 Sultans)
Defeated Mongol armies at battles of Kili, Jalandhar, Ravi, and Amroha
Built Siri Fort (made it his capital)

table.outcome: Raided Devgiri; loot used to usurp power from Jalaluddin
Defeated Vaghela King Karna; captured Kamal Devi & Malik Kafur
Defeated Hamir Deva; Nusrat Khan killed; Jauhar performed by Rajput women
Malik Kafur defeated Ramchandra Deva Yadava; given title Rai Raiyana
Malik Kafur defeated Kakatiya King Pratap Rudra Deva; Koh-i-noor diamond gifted

subtopics.name: PYQs – Khilji Dynasty
content: PYQ: Delhi Sultan known for Market Reforms = Alauddin Khalji (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Sultan who used Banjaras to transport grain = Alauddin Khalji (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Sultan who mobilised large standing army due to Mongol invasion = Both Alauddin Khalji & Muhammad Tughlaq (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Neither Malik Kafur nor Alauddin led campaigns against = Vijaya Nagara (Ans: C – SSC CGL)

subtopics.name: Ghiyas-ud-din Tughlaq (1320–1325)
content: Founder of Tughlaq Dynasty; was Governor of Punjab before becoming Sultan
Accepted title of Ghazi Malik
Introduced Postal Services and Irrigation System
Built city and strong fort of Tughlaqabad near Delhi
Amir Khusrau's famous work Tughlaq Nama deals with his rise
PYQ: Who constructed city of Tughlaqabad = Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)

subtopics.name: Muhammad Bin Tughlaq (1325–1351) – 'Mad King'
content: 1325 – New tax 'Kharaj-i-ala' on the Doab region
1327 – Changed name of Devgiri to Daulatabad & shifted capital from Delhi to Daulatabad
A severe famine broke out during his reign
1330 – Token Currency: Copper coins (failed experiment)
1333 – Ibn Batuta visited; wrote book Rehla; appointed Chief Qazi
Built fort of Adilabad, city of Jahanpanah, and Begumpuri mosque
1338 – Khorasan Project & Quarachill Expedition (1339)
Ziauddin Barani (1285–1358) was a political thinker of Delhi Sultanate during his reign
Appointed ordinary people to high posts: Aziz Khummar (wine distiller), Firuz Hajjam (barber), Manka Tabbakh (cook)
Established separate Agriculture department: Diwan-i-Amir Kohi #imp.2024
First Sultan to visit Ajmer shrine of Muinuddin Chishti
Harihara & Bukka established Vijayanagar Empire during his reign
PYQ: Delhi Sultan who appointed humble professionals to high posts = Muhammad Tughlaq (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Mughal capital transfer to Daulatabad ultimately failed = Both Statement 1 & 2 correct (Ans: C – SSC Phase XIII)

subtopics.name: Firoz Shah Tughlaq (1351–1388)
content: Longest reigning Tughlaq: 37 years
Created Diwan-i-Khairat (Department of Charity) and Diwan-i-Ishtiaq (Pension Department)
Built Feroz Shah Kotla Fort in 1354 AD
PYQ: Feroz Shah Kotla built in = 1354 (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Pair Ghiyasuddin Balban 1266–1287 & Firuz Shah Tughluq 1351–1388 = Both correct (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)

table.applicable_to: Import & Export tax on Commercial Goods #imp

subtopics.name: Departments of Delhi Sultanate
content: Diwan-i-Wizarat: Finance Department (headed by Wazir)
Diwan-i-Risalat: Religious, Foreign Affairs & Appeals
Diwan-i-Arz: Defence Department
Diwan-i-Insha/Ashraf: Correspondence / Media / News Department
Diwan-i-Amir Kohi: Agriculture Department (by Muhammad Bin Tughlaq) #imp.2024
Diwan-i-Mustakhraz: Revenue/Arrears Department (by Alauddin Khilji)
Diwan-i-Khairat: Department of Charity (by Firoz Shah Tughlaq)
Diwan-i-Ishtiaq: Pension Department
Diwan-i-Bandagan: Department of Slaves
Diwan-i-Qaza-i-Mamalik: Department of Justice
Diwan-i-Waqoof: Expense/Revenue Department

subtopics.name: Important Terms
content: Iqta: Land assignment system (Iltutmish); Khalisa = Sultan's land; Iqta = land given to nobles
Muqtis: In charge of Iqta; duty = lead military campaigns and maintain law & order (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
Bandagan: Military slaves purchased by the Sultan (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
Dehliwal: Coins minted in Delhi during reign of Tomara and Chauhan dynasties (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Tax on war loot during Delhi Sultanate = Khums (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
PYQ: Khums in Islamic taxation = One-fifth of war booty and hidden wealth (Ans: B – SSC CGL)

subtopics.name: Sayyid Dynasty (1414–1450)
content: Khizr Khan (1414–1421 AD)
Muizuddin Mubarak Shah (1421–1434 AD)
Yahya bin Ahmad Sirhindi wrote Tarikh-i-Mubarak Shahi #imp.2024
PYQ: Two dynasties that ruled Agra after Tughlaqs until 1526 = Sayyids and Lodis (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)

subtopics.name: Lodhi Dynasty (1451–1526)
content: Bahlol Lodhi (1451–1489): 1st Afghani Sultan in Delhi Sultanate (early rulers were Turks)
Did Demonetisation and launched Bahloli Coin
Sikander Lodhi / Nizam Khan (1489–1517): Introduced land unit 'Gaz-e-Sikandari' #imp
Established Agra City in 1504 AD #imp.2024
Shifted capital from Delhi to Agra in 1506 AD
Known by pen name 'Gulrukhi'; abolished Zakat & Octroi Duty on grains
Built Moth ki Masjid in Delhi (Double Dome Structure); Reimposed Jaziya
Ibrahim Lodhi (1517–1526): Defeated by Rana Sanga in Battle of Khatoli in 1517 #imp
1526 – First Battle of Panipat: Lost to Babur
Tomb of Ibrahim Lodhi is in Panipat (Haryana)
PYQ: Year Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi = 1526 (Ans: B – SSC CHSL)

subtopics.name: Vijayanagara Empire
content: Founded by Harihara & Bukka (during Muhammad Bin Tughlaq's reign)
Established by defeating Madurai Sultanate on behalf of Delhi Sultanate
Chronological order of rulers: Harihara I → Devaraya II → Krishnadeva Raya (Ans: C – SSC CGL)
Krishnadeva Raya – composed 'Amuktamalyada' (devotion to Lord Vishnu) (Ans: B – SSC CGL)
In Vijayanagara tradition, Rayas were called Narapati or 'lord of men' (Ans: D – SSC CGL)
Neither Iqta nor Mansabdari system was prevalent under Vijayanagara Empire (Ans: C – SSC CGL)
Amaram = Military fief in Vijayanagara administration (Ans: B – SSC CGL)
Lotus Palace shows influence of Persian (Indo-Islamic) architecture (Ans: B – SSC CGL)
Sacred center of city was south of Tungabhadra river

subtopics.name: Battle of Talikota (1565)
content: Fought on January 23, 1565
Aliya Rama Raya defeated by coalition of Deccan Sultanates (Bijapur, Ahmadnagar, Golconda, Bidar)
Vijayanagara collapsed; capital Hampi destroyed
PYQ: Confederation that defeated Vijayanagara at Talikota = Deccan Sultanates (Ans: D – Steno Exam)
PYQ: Chief commander who defeated Krishnadeva Raya at Raichur = Adil Shah of Bijapur (Ans: D – Steno Exam)

subtopics.name: Deccan Sultanates – Important Facts
content: Gol Gumbaz (tomb of Mohammed Adil Shah) is in Karnataka (Ans: C – SSC CHSL)
Golconda Fort is in Telangana (NOT Rajasthan); Bharatpur Fort is in Rajasthan (NOT Telangana) (Ans: D – SSC CHSL)
Marco Polo described pepper trade in Malabar during his return from China (Ans: C – SSC CGL)

subtopics.name: Babur (1526–1530)
content: Founder of Mughal Empire in India; related to Timur (father's side) & Genghis Khan (mother's side)
Real Name: Zahir-ud-din Muhammad Babur
Birth in Farghana (Uzbekistan); succeeded father in 1494; seized Kabul in 1504 #imp
Wife: Maham Begum, Aisha Sultan; Sister: Khanzada Begum
1st attack – Bhera; Autobiography: Baburnama (in Turkish)
Popularised Gunpowder; Cavalry & artillery tactics: Tulughma and Araba (effective use of Cannons)
Built Aram Bagh (oldest Mughal Garden) in 1528 in Agra
Introduced Charbagh (symmetrically laid out gardens) – inspired by Persian paradise garden concept
Death: 26 Dec 1530; Tomb: 1st at Arambagh in Agra, later moved to Kabul
First Mughal Emperor to be buried outside India (Ans: A – SSC CGL)
battles: 1529 – Ghaghra: Defeated Army of Bengal and Bihar

subtopics.name: Humayun (1530–1540 / 1555–1556)
content: Real Name: Mirza Nasir-ud-Din Muhammad (eldest son of Babur) #imp
Wives: Bega Begum, Hamida Banu Begum
Brothers: Kamran Mirza, Hindal Mirza, Askari Mirza & Khalil Mirza
Humayun controlled Delhi, Agra & Central India; Kamran controlled Afghanistan & Punjab #imp.2024
Title: Insan-i-Kamil (Perfect Man)
1531 – Devra: Humayun defeated Mahmud Lodi #imp
1539 – Chausa: Sher Shah Suri won #imp.2024
1540 – Kannauj/Belgram: Sher Shah Suri won #imp
1555 – Sirhind: Defeated Sikander Shah Suri #imp
Sister Gul Badan Begum wrote Humayun-Nama
Brought painters Mir Sayyid Ali and Abdal Samad (famous during Akbar's reign)
Built new city at Delhi named Dinpanah (on bank of River Yamuna)
Built Sher-e-Mandal (Library)
Widow Biga Begum built Humayun's Tomb (UNESCO site)
Humayun gave special patronage to Persian art and literature (Ans: A – Steno Exam)
PYQ: Humayun's Tomb first major Mughal garden tomb; inspired Taj Mahal design – Both A & R true, R explains A (Ans: A – SSC CGL)

subtopics.name: Key Facts – Sher Shah Suri
content: Real Name: Farid Khan
Title 'Sher Shah' accepted after Battle of Chausa
Founded Second Afghan Empire (Ans: B – SSC CGL)
Introduced Rupaya (1 Re = 178 grain Silver) #imp
Malik Muhammad Jaisi wrote Padmavat during his reign
Died in Battle of Kalinjer #imp
Tomb in Sasaram, Bihar #imp
Built Old Fort (Qila-e-Kuhana) and GT Road (Sadak-e-Azam-Uttarpath)
Empire divided into Sarkars; Chief Shiqdar (law & order) & Chief Munsif (judge) in charge of each sarkar
Mauza (village) was the lowest level of administration
Improved land revenue with Zabti-i-Har-Sal (land assessment every year)
Introduced new silver coins called Dam
Built Sarais (lodging/post offices)
Maintained personal royal force called Khasa Kail
Administrative system became the model for Akbar (Ans: C – SSC CGL)
Sur Dynasty ruled India for only 15 years (Ans: A – SSC CHSL)

subtopics.name: Akbar – Overview
content: Born 15th Oct 1542 in Amarkot #imp
Real Name: Abul-Fatah Jalal-ud-din Muhammad Akbar
Father/Mother: Humayun / Hamida Banu
Wife: Ruqaiya Begum, Jodha Bai (Heer Kunwari or Harka Bai) #imp
Official Language of Mughal Empire: Persian
Coronation at Kalanaur (Punjab) by Bairam Khan in 1556 (age 13) #imp.2024
Tomb of Akbar in Sikandra (UP) – built by Jahangir (1605–1613) #imp
Mansab System introduced by Todar Mal: Min Rank=10; Max Rank=10000
Court divided into Diwan-i-Aam and Diwan-i-Khas
1556–60: Ruled under Bairam Khan's regency; Bairam became Wakil with title Khan-i-Khanam

subtopics.name: Battles of Akbar
content: 1556 – 2nd Battle of Panipat: Akbar defeated Hemu (Sher Shah Suri's wazir)
1567 – Battle of Ascetics (Thanesar): Fought near Thanesar; Akbar won
1573 – Gujarat: Defeated Muzaffar Shah III; Akbar built Buland Darwaza at Fatehpur Sikri in 1575
Akbar founded city of Fatehpur Sikri (near Agra) in honour of Sufi Saint Sheikh Salim Chishti
1575 – Battle of Tukaroi (Bajhaura/Mughulmaar): Defeated Daud Khan Karrani (Bengal); Treaty of Katak
1576 – Battle of Haldighati (Mewar): Defeated Maharana Pratap; Mughal army led by Man Singh

chapters.chapter: Important Female Rulers of Medieval India

table.key_facts: First & last woman Muslim ruler of Delhi; wore Quba & Qula; commander Yaqut; married Altunia
Wife of Jahangir; exercised real political power; farmans & coins in her name; 'Nur Jahan Junta'
Brave Gond queen; fought Mughal general Asaf Khan; chose martyrdom over surrender
Regent of Ahmadnagar; heroically resisted Mughal invasions; key figure in Deccan politics

subtopics.name: Indo-Islamic Architecture
content: Features: domes, minarets, pointed arches, marble or red sandstone; NO ribbed vaults or flying buttresses
Qutub Minar (73 m, Red & buff Sandstone) – Indo-Islamic style
Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque – India's 1st Mosque (Delhi, 1192–1198 AD)
Tughlaqabad Fort – built by Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq
Siri Fort – built by Alauddin Khilji (his capital)
Humayun's Tomb (UNESCO) – first major Mughal garden tomb; introduced charbagh & double-dome style
Feroz Shah Kotla Fort – built 1354 AD (also called home of djinns)
Buland Darwaza at Fatehpur Sikri – built by Akbar (1575) after Gujarat conquest

subtopics.name: Mughal Gardens
content: Mughal style gardens inspired by Persian idea of paradise gardens (Ans: B – SSC CGL)
Babur built Aram Bagh (oldest Mughal Garden) in 1528 in Agra
Babur introduced Charbagh (symmetrical, four-part garden design)

table.author: Al-Biruni
table.notes: Written during Mahmud of Ghazni's time

table.author: Minhaj-ul-Siraj
table.notes: Dedicated to Nasiruddin Mahmud; praises Razia Sultan

table.author: Ibn Batuta
table.notes: Account of travel to India; visited under Muhammad Bin Tughlaq

table.author: Malik Muhammad Jaisi
table.notes: Written during Sher Shah Suri's reign

table.author: Krishnadeva Raya
table.notes: Devotion to Lord Vishnu; poetic richness (Ans: B – SSC CGL)

subtopics.name: Provincial Architecture
content: Bengal and Jaunpur developed distinctive architectural styles distinct from Delhi Sultanate
Regional styles often blended Islamic structures with local artistic traditions
PYQ: Statements on provincial architecture – Only 1 and 2 correct (Ans: B – Steno Exam)

pyqs.question: Chahamana ruler who defeated Ghori in 1191 = Prithviraja III
Prithviraj Chauhan's reign ended in = 1192 AD
Statement – Qutbuddin Aibak founded Slave Dynasty after Muhammad Ghori's death = Only 1 correct
Qutub Minar – Indo-Islamic style pair = Correct
Qutub Minar red sandstone tower base diameter = 14.32 metres
Iltutmish succeeded Aibak, later followed by daughter Razia = Only 1
Chahalgani formed during reign of = Iltutmish
Only woman monarch of Delhi Sultanate (1236–1240) = Razia Sultana
Chronicler who acknowledged Razia's capability = Minhaj-us-Siraj
Chronological order: Qutb-ud-din Aibak → Iltutmish → Razia Sultan → Balban
Delhi Sultan known for Market Reforms = Alauddin Khalji
Sultan who used Banjaras to transport grain = Alauddin Khalji
Alauddin admin reforms + Muhammad Bin Tughlaq capital transfer failed = Both correct
Sultan who appointed humble professionals to high posts = Muhammad Tughlaq
Who constructed city of Tughlaqabad = Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq
14th century traveller who described bamboo fortifications = Ibn Battuta
Feroz Shah Kotla Fort built in = 1354
Two dynasties ruling Agra after Tughlaqs until 1526 = Sayyids and Lodis
Bandagan in Persian = Military slaves purchased by the Sultan
Tax levied on war loot during Delhi Sultanate = Khums
Khums in Islamic taxation = One-fifth of war booty and hidden wealth
Coins minted in Delhi during Tomar and Chauhan reign = Dehliwal
Dehliwal coins minted by = Tomaras
Main duty of Muqtis in iqta = lead military campaigns and maintain law & order
Pair: Ghiyasuddin Balban 1266–1287 & Firuz Shah Tughluq 1351–1388 = Both correct
True arch and dome in India first introduced by = Delhi Sultanate
Confederation that defeated Vijayanagara at Talikota = Deccan Sultanates
Krishnadeva Raya's work renowned for devotion to Vishnu = Amuktamalyada
Narapati or 'lord of men' in Vijayanagara = Rayas
Correct sequence of Vijayanagara rulers = Harihara I → Devaraya II → Krishnadeva Raya
Amaram in Vijayanagara = Military fief
Neither Iqta nor Mansabdari prevalent under = Vijayanagara Empire
Lotus Palace shows influence of = Persian (Indo-Islamic) architecture
Chief commander who defeated Krishnadeva Raya at Raichur = Adil Shah of Bijapur
Gol Gumbaz (tomb of Mohammed Adil Shah) is in = Karnataka
On mother's side Mughals descended from = Genghis Khan
Babur of Mongol & Timurid descent, founded Mughal Empire defeating Ibrahim Lodi = Only 1
Babur took control of Kabul in = 1504 CE
Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi at First Battle of Panipat in = 1526
Ruler known for effective use of cannons in early 16th century = Babur
Mughal style gardens inspired by = Persian idea of paradise gardens
First Mughal Emperor buried outside India = Babur
Humayun's Tomb introduced charbagh & double-dome style; inspired Taj Mahal = Both A & R true, R explains A
Mughal ruler who gave special patronage to Persian art = Humayun
Whose admin system became model for Akbar = Sher Shah Suri
Dynasty that ruled India for only 15 years = Sur
Ruler who declared himself Sultan and founded Second Afghan Empire = Sher Shah Suri
Marco Polo described pepper trade in = Malabar
Decline of Timurid power in 2nd half of 15th century = Internal wars due to partition
